zh.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem是Heinric van Chaem的兒子，於大約1375年出生。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem是Heinric van Chaem的儿子，于大约1375年出生。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Chaem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish: from the personal name Chaim, from Hebrew ḥayim ‘life’; compare Haim and Heim .

variants.name: Haim
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Dror, Haim, Mazal, Mendel, Shimon, Yitzhak.  French Alain, Jacques, Marcel.
South German (mainly Austria) and Jewish: variant of   Heim  .
In some cases possibly a shortened form of English Haime (see Haimes ).

nameMeanings.name: Emondt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Normand, Armand, Marcel, Pierre, Yves, Adelard, Benoit, Camille, Cecile, Cyr, Fernand.
French (Émond): from a personal name derived from ancient Germanic Haimo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. Compare Aymond and Hemond , and also Amo and Emmons .

variants.name: Amo
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aymond
meanings: From an Old French name, Raimund, of Germanic origin, from ragin ‘advice, decision’ + mund ‘protector’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. Subsequently it dropped out of use, but was revived in the middle of the 19th century, together with several other given names of Old English and Norman origin.

variants.name: Hemond
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Andre, Fernand, Gaetan, Lucienne, Marcel.
French (Hémond): variant of Émond (see Emond ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

en.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem was born about 1375 as the son of Heinric van Chaem. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem est né environ 1375, fils de Heinric van Chaem. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem wurde um 1375, als Sohn von Heinric van Chaem geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem è nato il circa 1375 come figlio di Heinric van Chaem. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaemは約1375年にHeinric van Chaemの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem(은)는 대략 1375년 Heinric van Chaem의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem nasceu aproximadamente 1375 filho de Heinric van Chaem. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem родился около 1375 г. Его отца звали Heinric van Chaem. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Emondt Henrickzone van Chaem nació aproximadamente 1375 como hijo de Heinric van Chaem. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
